name: Shaw AFB
tagline: Home of the F-16 'Swamp Fox' wing and U.S. Army Central, in central South Carolina.
_note: Air Force fighter base + joint HQ (Sumter, central SC). CONUS full template (BAH MHA SC263 Sumter/Shaw AFB; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 20th Fighter Wing (F-16); also hosts HQ U.S. Army Central (USARCENT) and Ninth Air Force elements. Medical: 20th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (shaw.af.mil, 20 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). South Carolina is TRICARE East. Affordable. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 29150 (Sumter).

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Shaw Air Force Base is home to the 20th Fighter Wing, the 'Swamp Foxes,' flying F-16s, and it also hosts the headquarters of U.S. Army Central (USARCENT), making it a joint hub despite being an Air Force base.
It's in Sumter in central South Carolina, an affordable area near Columbia, the state capital.

data.subtitle: The Sumter area is affordable and family-friendly, with Columbia nearby. The main choice is in-town versus the suburbs, with on-base housing also available.
intro: Central SC is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well. On-base housing is available; many also live off base in Sumter and the surrounding area.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Sumter and (for some) the Columbia metro to the west.

recommendations.advice: Sumter surrounds the base with affordable housing and the shortest commute.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the affordable local market.
The Columbia metro is about 45 minutes west for big-city shopping, dining, and an airport.
The area is affordable, so BAH tends to go far and many families buy.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Shaw AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Shaw AFB has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA SC263 (Sumter/Shaw AFB)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Sumter School District serves the area around the base.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via area network hospitals (and Columbia)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Sumter's affordable market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable central SC market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Sumter's affordability makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the office's read on Sumter and the Columbia area before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and South Carolina vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Sumter School District early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 20th Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.q: What is Shaw AFB known for?
data.a: It's home to the 20th Fighter Wing (F-16 'Swamp Foxes') and also hosts the headquarters of U.S. Army Central, making it a joint hub.
On-base housing is available; many live off base in Sumter. The Columbia metro is about 45 minutes west for more amenities.
Yes. Central South Carolina is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well.
Shaw is MHA SC263 (Sumter/Shaw AFB). Use the calculator above for your grade.
